free_text: Neutron capture by Ru: Neutron cross sections of
96,102,104Ru and gamma-ray spectroscopy in the
decays of 97,103,105Ru
Samples of Ru metal were irradiated in
the Oregon State University TRIGA reactor
Ru metal (sponge or powder) or RuO2 powder of
natural isotopic abundance ranging from 5 to 20 mg
Gamma rays were observed with a high-purity Ge
detector with nominal volume of 169 cm3, efficiency
of 35% compared with NaI at 1332 keV and resolution
of 1.68 keV at 1332 keV. Source to detector distances
for the cross-section measurements were generally 10
to 20 cm,
Total uncertainty consisting of uncertainty
in detection efficiency below 200 keV
in detection efficiency above 200 keV
Included are also uncertainties in
- isotopic abundance
- half-life
- flux determination
- decay scheme
Compiled by S.H.
